ccel.title: NPNF2-06. Jerome: The Principal Works of St. Jerome

source.title: NPNF2-06. Jerome: The Principal Works of St. Jerome
NPNF2-06. Jerome: The Principal Works of St. Jerome

verses.text: He complains that even now he has not received Augustine’s letter and asks him to send him a copy of it.
Popular rumour, he declares, credits Augustine with a deliberate suppression of the letter in order that he may seem to win an easy victory over his opponent.
Jerome next deals with Augustine’s denial of having made a written attack upon him and concludes by refusing for the present all discussion of points of criticism.
The date of the letter is 403 a.d.
See Augustine, vol. i., Letter LXXII.
